title: Hours after wedding, Indian-origin Delta pilot Dave Fiji killed in US helicopter crash
content: An Indian-origin pilot, Dave Fiji, tragically died in a helicopter crash just hours after his wedding in Georgia. The crash also claimed the life of the helicopter pilot, while his new bride, Jesni, survived and is recovering. Dave had expressed concerns about poor visibility before the flight, which was a special send-off after the ceremony.

title: Hormuz crisis fallout: How Indian refiners are adjusting to new crude oil mix to maximise output
content: The ongoing geopolitical turmoil has affected supplies from several key oil-producing regions that traditionally cater to India, requiring refiners to process crude varieties that were not part of their usual feedstock mix.

title: RCB's rise across IPL, WPL feels closer to the Manchester City model
content: Royal Challengers Bengaluru's journey from perennial underachievers to champions is complete. With back-to-back IPL titles and two WPL crowns in three seasons, RCB have evolved from a star-driven franchise into a system-driven team. Their journey from one of the teams making up the numbers mirrors the transformation - and success - of Manchester City in England.

title: Gross GST revenues rise 3.2% to over Rs 1.94 lakh crore in May
content: India's Goods and Services Tax collections in May surpassed Rs 1.94 lakh crore, marking a 3.2% year-on-year increase. While domestic revenue saw a slight dip, robust growth in import-linked taxes fueled the overall rise. Several states reported healthy SGST collection increases, though Delhi experienced a significant decline.

title: Google AI CEO has a message for laid off engineers at Meta, Amazon, Block, and other companies
content: Google DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis argues companies cutting engineers due to AI are misguided. He believes increased productivity should fuel more projects, not layoffs. Hassabis sees AI as an opportunity to expand ambitions, not shrink workforces, and is eager to hire talent shed by rivals for new ventures like drug discovery and game design.

title: West Bengal cabinet expansion: 35 ministers take oath in Suvendu Adhikari govt
content: Governor R N Ravi administered the oath, bringing the total strength of the Council of Ministers to 41 under Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari's administration. The expansion marks a significant reshuffle as the state government moves to strengthen its administrative team and governance across various departments.

title: Russia imposes aviation fuel export ban until November 30
content: Russia has banned aviation fuel exports until November 30 to ensure domestic supply stability amid falling refinery output. This move follows Ukrainian attacks on energy facilities, which have reduced Russia's fuel processing capacity. The restriction aims to prevent shortages and maintain domestic market equilibrium.

title: 'Amazon would collapse if run like New York City': Jeff Bezos takes swipe at Mamdani
content: Amazon founder Jeff Bezos has endorsed New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani's new government efficiency initiative, COGE. This comes after their public disagreement on taxing the wealthy. Bezos, who previously criticized city spending, now supports Mamdani's plan to make government work smarter, suggesting savings could benefit lower earners.

title: US pushes new Israel-Lebanon ceasefire plan; Trump revises Iran proposal
content: The US has proposed a plan to de-escalate tensions between Israel and Lebanon, urging Hezbollah to halt attacks in exchange for Israel refraining from escalation in Beirut. Meanwhile, President Trump has reportedly sent back a proposed agreement with Iran for further revisions, seeking tougher provisions and guarantees against nuclear weapons.

title: IPL 2026 prize money: How much did champions RCB and runners-up GT earn this season?
content: Royal Challengers Bengaluru secured the IPL 2026 title and a ₹20 crore prize. Gujarat Titans finished as runners-up, earning ₹13 crore. Rajasthan Royals received ₹7 crore for their third-place finish. Sunrisers Hyderabad took home ₹6.5 crore. The total prize pool for the four playoff teams was over ₹46.5 crore, concluding a successful season.

title: Gujarat Titans players evacuated after team bus suffers short circuit; all safe
content: A disappointing IPL final defeat was followed by a tense off-field scare for Gujarat Titans as a short circuit in the team bus left players stranded for nearly an hour. The incident capped a gruelling week of travel, weather disruptions and high-pressure knockout matches. GT lost the final by five wickets against RCB at the Narendra Modi Stadium on Sunday.

title: Anthropic CEO, who warns of AI-led mass layoffs, calls them a necessity
content: Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei has shifted from just warning about AI-driven job losses to insisting the policy response to them is a bipartisan necessity. Speaking to WSJ at Davos, the man behind Claude said "ideology will not survive the nature of this technology." His comments arrive as Anthropic chases a reported $900 billion IPO valuation and as rivals like Demis Hassabis and Yann LeCun openly question his doomsday framing.

title: As ACs run nonstop in 45°C heat, are Delhi homes facing a growing fire hazard?
content: Delhi-NCR's sweltering heat is pushing ACs to their limits, sparking a rise in dangerous fires. Faulty wiring, overloaded circuits, and poor maintenance are increasingly causing AC-related blazes, with recent fatal incidents highlighting the urgent need for awareness. Experts warn that ignoring early warning signs like burning smells or flickering lights can have devastating consequences.

title: Rajat Patidar's four-word post after joining Dhoni, Rohit with back-to-back IPL titles
content: Royal Challengers Bengaluru secured their second consecutive IPL title, defeating Gujarat Titans in the IPL 2026 final. Captain Rajat Patidar became the third skipper to achieve back-to-back titles, joining Dhoni and Sharma. Patidar highlighted the team's dominant performance and praised Virat Kohli's invaluable influence.

title: 'Sitting inside CBSE': Rahul's 'beware of pickpockets' warning amid OSM row
content: Gandhi on Sunday stepped up his attack on Prime Minister Narendra Modi over the CBSE OSM controversy, accusing him of ignoring the concerns of 18.5 lakh students allegedly affected by irregularities in the board's answer-sheet scanning process. In a post on X, the leader of opposition in the Lok Sabha said the Prime Minister found time to speak on a range of issues during his monthly Mann Ki Baat address, but not about students whose answer sheets were allegedly not assessed properly.

title: IPL 2026: How every piece of the puzzle fell into place for RCB
content: Every major RCB win in IPL 2026 featured a different hero. Sometimes it was Kohli. Sometimes Patidar. Sometimes Padikkal. Sometimes David. Sometimes Krunal. Sometimes Bhuvneshwar. Sometimes it was the bowlers operating as a collective unit. That was the defining characteristic of this championship side. The first IPL trophy ended one of the league's longest waits. The second confirmed something even more important. Royal Challengers Bengaluru are no longer a franchise chasing history. They may well be on their way to creating one.

title: What's the outlook for Nifty this week? Check list of top stock recommendations
content: Stock market recommendations: Nuvama Wealth Management Ltd, and R R Kabel Ltd have been picked as the top stocks to buy in the June 1-June 5, 2026 week by Sudeep Shah, Head - Technical Research and Derivatives, SBI Securities:

title: RCB's new era, Kohli's old magic: Defending champions outclass GT in IPL final
content: Virat Kohli produced a masterful unbeaten 75 after Royal Challengers Bengaluru's (RCB) pace attack dismantled Gujarat Titans at the Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad on Sunday. Rajat Patidar's men secured a second successive IPL title with a commanding five-wicket victory. Rajat Patidar also became only the third captain to have won back-to-back IPL titles after MS Dhoni and Rohit Sharma.

title: Gold price prediction today: Will gold prices see limited upside? Key levels to watch out
content: Gold price prediction today: Momentum in gold prices remains weak, and markets will track the US-Iran developments for cues, says Manav Modi, Senior Analyst, Commodity Research at Motilal Oswal Financial Services Ltd.

title: Gujarat's Halpati Housing Scheme explained: Check eligibility and how to apply
content: Gujarat's Halpati Housing Scheme offers Rs 1.2 lakh aid to Talaviya and Nayak families in southern districts for home construction. Beneficiaries rural areas of southern Gujarat can apply. No income limit applies, and funds are disbursed in phases directly to bank accounts. Essential documents include age and caste proof, birth certificate, and ration card.

title: Norway Chess: Gukesh, Praggnanandhaa, Divya all suffer classical defeats
content: Round 6 of Norway Chess brought a dramatic reversal for India as D Gukesh, R Praggnanandhaa, and Divya Deshmukh all suffered costly classical defeats in the reverse fixtures. Vincent Keymer outplayed Gukesh, Wesley So overcame Praggnanandhaa in a superior endgame, and Women’s World Champion Ju Wenjun defeated Divya. Magnus Carlsen returned to winning ways against Alireza Firouzja, while Koneru Humpy lost her Armageddon tie-break, capping a difficult day for India.

title: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi can't use his biggest prize - Here's why
content: Fifteen-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi dominated IPL 2026, scoring 776 runs and winning multiple awards, including the Orange Cap and MVP. Despite winning a Tata Sierra, he cannot drive it due to his age. Sooryavanshi emphasized learning to adapt to pressure and situations, and the importance of fitness for a long career.

title: Commercial LPG gets costlier: How much will a 19-kg cylinder cost? Check city-wise rates
content: Commercial LPG cylinder prices saw a hike across India from June 1, with a 19-kg cylinder increasing by Rs 42 to Rs 53.50 in major cities. This revision impacts businesses like hotels and restaurants, while domestic cooking gas prices remain unchanged. The increase coincides with efforts to bolster India's fuel security amidst global supply concerns.

title: JEE Advanced results 2026 announced: Shubham Kumar secures AIR 1 with 330 marks
content: The Indian Institute of Technology, Roorkee has declared the JEE Advanced 2026 results and released the final answer key for Paper 1 and Paper 2. Shubham Kumar secured AIR 1 with 330 marks out of 360, followed by Kabir Chhillar with 329 marks and Jatin Chahar with 319 marks. Of the 179,694 candidates who appeared for the examination, 56,880 qualified, including 10,107 female candidates. Results are available on the official website.

title: Hormuz to home: How Middle East conflict is rewriting your kitchen math
content: Geopolitical tensions in the Middle East are directly impacting household budgets, driving up prices for everyday essentials like cooking oil, milk, and bread. Soaring global crude oil prices and a weakening rupee are increasing import, fuel, and transportation costs, leading to a domino effect on consumer goods and even healthcare services.

title: Commercial LPG gets costlier: Cylinder sees average hike of Rs 47.75 across cities
content: Commercial LPG prices have risen by up to Rs 53.50 per cylinder nationwide, effective June 1st. This adjustment aims to bolster fuel reserves amid West Asia conflict concerns. The government is pushing for 30 days of LPG reserves and intensifying anti-hoarding measures, assuring sufficient fuel stocks despite import reliance.

title: AP EAMCET 2026 result out: Here's how to check scorecards and admission ranks
content: The AP EAMCET 2026 result is expected to be released today by JNTU Kakinada on the official website. Candidates can access their scorecards using their registration number and EAMCET hall ticket number. The university is also likely to publish the rank list, which will be used during counselling and seat allotment. Rankings will be based entirely on AP EAPCET marks, with a normalisation process applied to ensure fairness across examination shifts.

title: Fresh blow to TMC as 9 councillors quit, Diamond Harbour board dissolved
content: Diamond Harbour municipality's board has been dissolved after nine Trinamool Congress councillors resigned, citing financial irregularities and intimidation. The civic body, located in Abhishek Banerjee's constituency, now falls under administrative control with the sub-divisional officer appointed as administrator. This development marks another blow to the TMC following recent election losses.

title: Virat Kohli meets Vaibhav Sooryavanshi after RCB's title win - Watch
content: Virat Kohli led Royal Challengers Bengaluru to their second consecutive IPL title, sealing the victory with a signature six. Post-match, Kohli shared a heartwarming moment with 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, who dominated the awards ceremony with multiple accolades after a record-breaking season.

title: Oil prices: Crude jumps over 2% after Israel intensifies attacks in Lebanon
content: Oil prices surged over 2% as Israel ordered troop deployments into southern Lebanon, escalating tensions with Hezbollah. This renewed fighting raises concerns for a US-Iran ceasefire extension and regional stability, impacting global energy supply outlooks.  Mines in the Strait of Hormuz further complicate efforts to restore shipping traffic.

title: Top stocks to buy: Stock recommendations for June 1, 2026 week - check list
content: Stock market recommendations: Astra Microwave, and Shaily Engineering Plastics have been recommended by Motilal Oswal Wealth Management Research Desk as the top stocks to buy for the week starting June 1, 2026:

title: Rashmika Mandanna documents Japan trip with Vijay Deverakonda in loved-up social media dump
content: Newlyweds Rashmika Mandanna and Vijay Deverakonda are enjoying married life, recently sharing glimpses of their Japan trip. Mandanna posted photos from their getaway, featuring her in traditional attire and exploring local sights. The couple, who married in Udaipur, also sparked fan attention with a subtle detail on Deverakonda's jewelry. Their next film together, 'Ranabaali', is set for release.

title: DK Shivakumar and Siddaramaiah head to Delhi to finalise new Karnataka team
content: Karnataka Congress is preparing its new government team. DK Shivakumar, the Chief Minister-elect, will meet the high command in Delhi to finalize the first set of ministers. A new state party chief will also be appointed. The oath-taking ceremony is scheduled for Wednesday. Cabinet formation will occur in two phases.

title: 'One felt nice ... ': Virat Kohli dons special T-shirt, dances with Anushka
content: Royal Challengers Bengaluru secured their second consecutive IPL title, defeating Gujarat Titans by five wickets. Virat Kohli, instrumental in the win with an unbeaten 75, celebrated joyfully with wife Anushka Sharma, wearing a special "we did it twice" T-shirt. Kohli highlighted the team's depth and confidence in their successful title defense.

title: Demolition drive starts in Delhi's Shalimar Bagh to widen road
content: A demolition drive targeting unauthorized constructions in Shalimar Bagh's Haiderpur area commenced Sunday, following Supreme Court validation of road widening plans. Officials confirmed the action complies with court orders to reclaim government land for Road No. 320, crucial for connectivity. The drive aims to remove encroachments that have significantly narrowed the road, impacting emergency services.

title: 'Panchayat' Season 5 star Chandan Roy aka Vikas decodes Phulera’s next chapter
content: Fans eagerly await Panchayat Season 5, with actor Chandan Roy, who plays Vikas, hinting at an exciting new chapter. He described the upcoming season as 'Bigger, better and more beautiful,' promising a deeper exploration of characters and their evolving relationships. The show's authentic portrayal of rural Indian life continues to resonate universally, making Phulera's return highly anticipated.

title: Commercial LPG prices hiked amid West Asia conflict
content: Commercial LPG prices have been hiked again amid ongoing global tensions, marking the fourth increase since the West Asia conflict began. While businesses and vulnerable groups feel the impact, domestic LPG prices remain unchanged.

title: JEE Advanced 2026 results: Shubham Kumar secures AIR 1; over 56,000 qualify
content: A total of 56,880 candidates — 10,107 of whom were female — passed in the in JEE Advanced 2026 entrance examination, which is considered as one of the country's toughest

title: RCB’s second IPL crown sets Vizag’s Beach Road alight for Virat Kohli
content: RCB fans pour onto Visakhapatnam’s Beach Road to mark a second IPL title and turn the night into a tribute to Virat Kohli

title: Prof. Mavoothu D. appointed Mahatma Gandhi University Vice-Chancellor in-charge
content: Prof. Mavoothu currently serves as the Dean of the Faculty of Social Sciences at CUSAT and the Dean of the Faculty of Management Studies at Kerala University

title: Marina Blue Flag beach project near Vivekananda House nearing completion
content: Facilities such as meditation centre, reading room, selfie points, children’s play areas, separate play equipment for persons with disabilities, and breastfeeding rooms have been set up at the beach

title: West Bengal Cabinet expansion LIVE: 35 MLAs take oath
content: Swapan Dasgupta, Dudh Kumar Mondal, Deepak Burman, Manoj Oraon, Gouri Shankar Ghosh, Arjun Singh, Tapas Roy, Saradwat Mukherjee, Jagannath Chattopadhyay and Shankar Ghosh were among those sworn in as Cabinet Ministers

title: T.N. government announces zero-tolerance approach towards delays in sanctioning building plan approvals
content: State warns planning officials of disciplinary action for negligence and violation of transparency processes

title: 'Beware of pickpockets': Rahul slams Govt. over CBSE re-evaluation cost
content: A student may have to pay up to ₹2,000 just to ensure the accurate evaluation of their own answer sheet, said Rahul Gandhi. ‘Just imagine the revenue CBSE is generating when four lakh students have submitted such applications,’ he added

title: Sri Lankan Tamil girl killed after gang rams SUV into two-wheeler on Chennai’s Koyambedu flyover
content: A six-member gang first picked a fight with her and then assaulted her before driving the SUV into her two-wheeler, say the police

title: Woman shot at during morning walk in U.P.'s Etah, land dispute suspected
content: A medical officer said the woman had sustained a serious gunshot injury and was referred to a specialised medical centre in view of her critical condition

title: CUET-UG 2026 exam, rescheduled over Id, conducted smoothly across centres
content: The National Testing Agency (NTA) conducted the rescheduled Common University Entrance Test-Undergraduate 2026 examination in two shifts at centres across the country and overseas

title: Man, wanted in six criminal cases, shot dead by unidentified assailants in Bihar
content: The incident occurred between 9.45 p.m. and 10 p.m. on May 31, 2026, in the basement of the apartment where Govind Sharma was present, said Muzaffarpur SSP Kantesh Kumar Mishra

title: White Paper on Tamil Nadu Government’s finances is being finalised
content: While assuming office last month, CM Vijay said his govt. would release the document, alleging that the DMK govt. left behind an empty treasury. It is likely to be out in a few days, says an official

title: With departed souls in mind, fans in Bengaluru celebrate another IPL win in the ‘right spirit’
content: With police barriers around, they danced on the streets revelling in the team's remarkable success and their icon Virat Kohli, who smashed an unbeaten fifty to anchor the chase

title: Nepal has encroached on Indian territory as well: PM Balendra Shah
content: Nepal Prime Minister Balendra Shah’s remarks about border encroachment triggered a major political uproar in Parliament. The government later issued a clarification, citing technical factors related to shifting river boundaries.

title: BJP to host Nepal ruling party delegation in outreach to new govt.
content: The visit of the delegation assumes significance as it comes days after the cancellation of Foreign Secretary Vikram Misri’s proposed visit to Kathmandu

title: Bengaluru East Corporation seizes six vehicles for illegal dumping of waste along riverbank, FIR registered
content: Using fake identity cards, they allegedly collected waste and dumped it illegally in areas adjoining the Dakshina Pinakini river near Siddhartha Layout in the Kadugodi jurisdiction

title: FIFA World Cup 2026 | Why football’s most passionate fans may be in India
content: In Kerala, West Bengal and several other places across India, the FIFA World Cup is a homecoming — of fan clubs, midnight screenings, bike rallies and WhatsApp groups that spring back to life every four years

title: Signs emerge of a thaw in Vellappally-Satheesan ties
content: Thushar Vellappally calls on Satheesan at his residence in Desom, Aluva and Labour Minister Bindu Krishna visits Vellappally Natesan at his house in Kanichukulangara. Yogam has not worked against him at the hustings, says Thushar

title: What is changing for UPI and LPG from June 1, 2026? Everything to know
content: Some new changes to the UPI system and the LPG distribution system guidelines will be in effect from June 1, 2026.

title: 699 booked for drunk driving in Bengaluru during week-long police crackdown
content: The operation was carried out by personnel from all 53 traffic police stations in Bengaluru as part of efforts to improve road safety and reduce accidents caused by reckless driving.

title: Nepal has also encroached upon Indian territory, says PM Balendra Shah
content: Lawmakers from the Nepali Congress and the Nepal Communist Party demanded that the Prime Minister clarify where exactly Nepal has encroached upon Indian territory and sought the removal of his remarks from the record.

title: West Bengal Cabinet expansion: 35 more Ministers to be sworn in on June 1
content: With this expansion on June 1, Cabinet strength will rise to 41; along with seniority, the BJP is likely to give considerations to regional and community-wise representations

title: Rahul targets Centre over CBSE OSM row, alleges ‘fraud’ in scanning process
content: In a separate social media post, Congress communication chief Jairam Ramesh alleged a major data breach in the CBSE’s Class 12 examination system, claiming that answer sheets of nearly two million students were available in the public domain.

title: 15-day ultimatum to Rabri Devi to vacate Patna bungalow
content: RJD leaders say Minister Ram should question why government is repeatedly changing his residence, after he claimed Rabri Devi was refusing to vacate because he was from the Dalit community

title: Karnataka CM Siddaramaiah resigns, but won’t budge from State politics
content: Going down memory lane, he said he was an ‘accidental politician’, with none in the family being in politics

title: D.K. Shivakumar to be sworn-in as Karnataka Chief Minister at Glass House in Lok Bhavan on June 3
content: KPCC Working President G.C. Chandrashekar appeals to party workers not to come to venue to reduce inconvenience to people

title: Shivakumar, Siddaramaiah to take Karnataka Cabinet formation talks to Delhi
content: D.K. Shivakumar and outgoing Chief Minister Siddaramaiah expected make their choices known to the Congress central leadership

title: Five killed, at least 10 injured in road accident in Chickballapur
content: The incident occurred when the victims were returning home in an autorickshaw after completing their day’s work. They were daily-wage labourers

title: ‘Nexbax AI Index’ to redefine AI adoption in India
content: It argues that the metrics dominating AI development in the world are essentially useless for the billion-plus users across India and the Global South

title: U.P.'s 'cockroach man' floats CJP spin-off after drawing attention to Yamuna's plight
content: The main reason behind dressing up as a cockroach was to tap the sudden interest centred on the insect to highlight neglect of the Yamuna river, among other things, says  Deepak Sharma

title: Corruption crackdown: Rajasthan government dismisses 20 officials, suspends 332
content: Among those dismissed are a Rajasthan Administrative Service (RAS) officer and several officials from departments including medical, education, mining and public works.

title: Twisha Sharma death case: Daughters killed for dowry
content: In the past three weeks, 33-year-old Twisha Sharma’s death over alleged dowry harassment has seen alleged procedural lapses and institutional bias by  Bhopal Police. As her mother-in-law, a retired judge, slandered her daughter-in-law, a former model on TV channels, Twisha’s parents and sibling stood up against the influential in-laws. An exhausting timeline of the case and the debate it has sparked about walking out of a bad marriage

title: NSA Ajit Doval meets Myanmar President Aung Hlaing
content: Hlaing set to meet PM Modi on Monday; issues relating to border security and connectivity to be discussed during the Myanmarese leader’s trip to India

title: SIR enumeration phase begins in Odisha, Mizoram, Sikkim and Manipur
content: Enumeration forms must be submitted by June 28 to be included in draft electoral rolls; this is part of the third phase of the SIR, which will complete the process across the country except Himachal, J&K, Ladakh

title: Granite quarries in north Kerala under increased surveillance after NIA raids
content: Move amid unconfirmed reports that legally procured explosives could be diverted for anti-social activities; inspections to ensure compliance with explosives-handling norms

title: Lok Adalats, DLSAs have no authority to grant divorce: Allahabad High Court
content: The court, in an order passed on April 30, said the power to dissolve a marriage rests exclusively with family courts, while the authority of Lok Adalats is limited to facilitating settlements between parties, and it cannot render judicial decisions.

title: The persecution of consent
content: It is important for the law, courts, and the police to handle with a degree of sensitivity the question of consensual, underage relationships, simply because the tangle ensuing from this ‘offence’ has upended many young lives

title: Manoj Jarange Patil ends fast, demands separate Ministry for Maratha community
content: Maharashtra government representatives hand over a 12-point proposal to the Maratha reservation activist in connection with the reservation.

title: Sitting Vice-Chancellor on search panel raises conflict of interest concerns, says expert
content: The Department of Agriculture, through a notification dated May 14, constituted a search committee to recommend a panel of eligible candidates for the post of Vice-Chancellor of KSNUAHS, Shivamogga

title: 17-year-old from Bengaluru listed in Forbes 30 under 30
content: Soon after the launch, Divaa Uthkarsha’s Project Surya has reached over 4,02,000 individuals and directly supported 3,190 children living with Type 1 diabetes by raising over ₹20 lakh in funds

title: Gujarat CM participates in toy collection drive for underprivileged children
content: The campaign, inspired by Union Home Minister Amit Shah, aims to encourage children from well-off families to donate toys they no longer use to underprivileged children.

title: Bihar BJP’s Pandit Deendayal Upadhyaya mega training campaign-2026 concludes
content: Chief Minister Samrat Choudhary addressed the concluding session in Bhagalpur, while State President Sanjay Saraogi addressed training sessions in Motihari and Valmikinagar. Around 20,000 party workers across 52 organisational districts received Ideological and Organisational Training.

title: Suvendu Adhikari's Cabinet Expands, 35 BJP MLAs Take Oath As Ministers
content: After the induction of the 35 legislators into the council of ministers, the Bengal cabinet strength is now 41, three short of the maximum count of ministers that a government can have in the 294-member assembly.

title: 'Tapori Lal' vs 'Abhinandan Lal': BJP, Congress Trade Barbs In Madhya Pradesh
content: What began days ago with Patwari mockingly referring to the Chief Minister as "Mohan Lal Abhinandan Yadav" escalated dramatically when Mohan Yadav hit back from a public stage.

title: "Are We Nobody?" Pregnant Wife In Car, Bengaluru Man Blocks Governor's Convoy
content: Stuck in India's Silicon Valley, one frustrated citizen decided to resort to a Gandhian mode of protest.

title: After Abhishek Banerjee Attacked On Hometurf, A Moment Of Opposition Unity
content: Abhishek Banerjee was pushed and heckled, and stones and eggs were thrown at him on Saturday in Sonarpur near Kolkata on Saturday.

title: Indian-Origin Pilot Dies In US Chopper Crash. He Got Married Just Hours Ago
content: According to his father, Dave Fiji, a first officer for Delta Air Lines, had concerns about the weather before the takeoff.

title: Rain Relief For North India To End Tonight, Temperatures Will Rise Again
content: A Western Disturbance is a weather system that brings rain, thunderstorms, strong winds, and cooler temperatures to North India.

title: India Tweaks Solar Rules To Cut China Dependence, Manufacturers Divided
content: Manufacturers say modules built using domestic cells are substantially more expensive than those using imported cells.

title: 'Gave Rs 7 Lakh Dowry, They Wanted Car Too': Lucknow Influencer Found Dead
content: During their marriage, her family claimed that they had given Rs 7 lakh in cash and various other household items as 'gifts'.

title: US-Iran war live updates: US Launches 'Self-Defence' Strikes On Iranian Drone Sites
content: US-Iran war live updates: The remark by Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf came as reports suggested US President Donald Trump has sent back a revised, tougher peace proposal

title: Man From PoK Crosses Line Of Control To Meet J&K Woman He Met Online
content: At around 9:30 am on Sunday, troops spotted a man approaching from the other side of the Line of Control (LoC) in the Silikote area of Uri. He was detained soon after crossing into the Indian side.

title: Picnic Turns Deadly, 2 Teens Drown At Maharashtra Dam, 1 Body Recovered
content: Seven friends had set out for the Jayakwadi dam in Aurangabad district on Sunday, planning to spend the day exploring nearby tourist spots.

title: Siddaramaiah's Son Among Cabinet Aspirants As Karnataka Sees Change Of Guard
content: DK Shivakumar will be sworn in as chief minister on Wednesday after the Congress agreed to a power shift in Karnataka last week.

title: Argument In Chennai Bar Escalates Into Road Rage, Car Rams 2-Wheeler, Kills Woman
content: An altercation between two groups in a bar led to a road rage, killing an 18-year-old woman in Chennai

title: Malaysia Enforces Social Media Ban For Under-16, Platforms Must Verify Age
content: The rules require social media platforms to implement age-verification systems and block users under 16 from creating accounts. They apply to platforms with at least 8 million users, including Facebook, Instagram, TikTok and YouTube.

title: Did Iran's President Masoud Pezeshkian Resign Over Disagreement With IRGC? A Fact Check
content: This added to months of reports claiming tensions between the government and the Islamic Republic's military-security institutions.

title: What's P2P Lending? Why Banks, RBI Are Watching This Credit Trend Closely
content: The Reserve Bank of India regulates P2P lending platforms as NBFC-P2P entities and has introduced guardrails around exposure limits.

title: Indians remained 3rd largest Schengen visa applicants in 2025; numbers climb in tough travel year
content: CY 2025 saw 4% more Indians applying for Schengen visas than in 2024 . rejection rate also climbed from 15% to 15.8% for Indian applicants . soaring international airfares and the rupee's steep depreciation have together made foreign travel more expensive .

title: Stuff of champions: Virat Kohli, ruthless bowling attack power RCB to consecutive IPL crowns
content: royal Challengers Bengaluru beat Gujarat Titans by five wickets in a one-sided final at the Narendra Modi Stadium on Sunday night . 'King' Kohli smashed arshad Khan over long-on for six to finish things off with two overs to spare . the modern great brought up a 25-ball half-century, the fastest of his IPL career .
